---
title: "Compare pangenome structure to density of GWAS loci - third aproach"
author: "CIG @ MGI"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/Users/juanmacias/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/LawsonLab/JuanMacias/Postdoc/SMaHT/Pangenome_variation_vs_GWAS_Loci_Neurodegenerative_Disorders/")

# Set seed
set.seed(0)

# Load packages
library(ggplot2)
require(cowplot)
library(viridis)
library(tidyr)

theme_set(theme_cowplot())

# Set theme object
CS.THEME<-theme(
  panel.background = element_rect(fill="white"),
  panel.grid.major=element_line(color="gray90", linetype="solid"), #adds major grid lines
  panel.grid.minor=element_line(color="gray95", linetype="solid", size=0.65), #adds minor grid lines
  panel.border=element_rect(fill=NA, color="black", size=1.5, linetype="solid"), #draws a black border around plot
  axis.line=element_blank(),
  text=element_text(colour = "black", family= "Arial")
)

# Import custom common omics functions
source("/Users/juanmacias/Documents/GitHub/JuanMacias_General_Code/R_Scripts/General/OmicsPlottingFunctions.R")
```

```{r import pangenome data, include=FALSE}
# Set input file path for diversity metrics
## 24kb bins
InputFile.24Kb.Diversity<-"/Users/juanmacias/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/LawsonLab/JuanMacias/Postdoc/HPRC/Pangenome_Diversity_Association/CompareToVariantCallRate/Bins_24kb_Diversity_Metrics.bed"

# Read diversity metrics files
## 24Kb bins
DiversityMetrics.24Kb<-read.table(InputFile.24Kb.Diversity, header=FALSE, sep="\t", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
colnames(DiversityMetrics.24Kb)<-c("Chr", "Start", "Stop", "ShannonIndex.mean", "ShannonIndex.max", "ShannonIndex.min", "ShannonIndex.count", "NonRefAlleles.mean","NonRefAlleles.sum","NonRefAlleles.max","NonRefAlleles.min","TotalHaplotypes.mean","TotalHaplotypes.max","TotalHaplotypes.min")

# Convert "." to NA
DiversityMetrics.24Kb[DiversityMetrics.24Kb=="."]<-NA

# Convert to numeric
DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum<-as.numeric(DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum)

DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.mean<-as.numeric(DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.mean)

DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.max<-as.numeric(DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.max)

DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.min<-as.numeric(DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.min)

# Leave in only NonRefAlleles metrics
DiversityMetrics.24Kb<-DiversityMetrics.24Kb[,c(1:3, 8:11)]
```

```{r calculate weighted diversity metric}
# For the 9th column generate a 240Kb sliding window that averages the value of the 9th column at the ith position with with next 10 positions
DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum.mean<-rollmean(as.numeric(DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum), 10, fill = NA)

# Calculate the deviation of the 9th column from the mean and weight it by the mean
DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation<-100*(as.numeric(DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum)-DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum.mean)/DiversityMetrics.24Kb$NonRefAlleles.sum.mean
```

```{r plot distribution of diversity metric, include=TRUE}
DiversirtyThreshold<-50

# Plot distribution of diversity metric - 24Kb bins
ggplot(DiversityMetrics.24Kb, aes(x=NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation))+
  geom_histogram(fill="blue", color="blue", bins=100)+
  labs(title="Distribution of diversity metric - 24Kb bins", x="Weighted Non-reference allele count rate", y="Frequency")+
  CS.THEME+
  geom_vline(xintercept=DiversirtyThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")+
  geom_vline(xintercept=-DiversirtyThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")+
  # Add labels to denote the thresholds {Low, Moderate, High}
  annotate("text", x=50, y=-400, label="High", color="black", size=2.5)+
  annotate("text", x=-50, y=-400, label="Low", color="black", size=2.5)+
  annotate("text", x=0, y=-400, label="Medium", color="black", size=2.5)

# Plot cumulative distribution of diversity metric - 24Kb bins
ggplot(DiversityMetrics.24Kb, aes(x=NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation))+
  stat_ecdf(geom="step", color="blue")+
  labs(title="Cumulative distribution of diversity metric - 24Kb bins", x="Weighted Non-reference allele count rate", y="Cumulative probability")+
  CS.THEME+
  geom_vline(xintercept=DiversirtyThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")+
  geom_vline(xintercept=-DiversirtyThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")+
  # Add labels to denote the thresholds {Low, Moderate, High}
  annotate("text", x=50, y=-0.025, label="High", color="black", size=2.5)+
  annotate("text", x=-50, y=-0.025, label="Low", color="black", size=2.5)+
  annotate("text", x=0, y=-0.025, label="Medium", color="black", size=2.5)

```

```{r import associations GWAS loci data, include=TRUE}
# All associations file
GWAS_Data<-read.delim("gwas_catalog_v1.0.2-associations_e113_r2024-11-03.tsv", header=TRUE, sep="\t", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
## How many associations are there?
nrow(GWAS_Data)

# Neurodegenerative disorders associations file
GWAS_Data_ND<-read.delim("gwas-association-downloaded_2024-11-08-EFO_0005772-withChildTraits.tsv", header=TRUE, sep="\t", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)

## How many associations are there?
nrow(GWAS_Data_ND)

# Combine all associations based on STUDY.ACCESSION
GWAS_Data<-rbind(GWAS_Data, GWAS_Data_ND[!(GWAS_Data_ND$STUDY.ACCESSION %in% GWAS_Data$STUDY.ACCESSION),])
## How many associations are there?
nrow(GWAS_Data)


# Filter out P-values that are not significant (P.VALUE>5*10^-8) - full set
nrow(GWAS_Data[GWAS_Data$P.VALUE<=5*10^-8,])/nrow(GWAS_Data)
GWAS_Data<-GWAS_Data[GWAS_Data$P.VALUE<=5*10^-8,]
## How many associations are there?
nrow(GWAS_Data)

# Filter out P-values that are not significant (P.VALUE>5*10^-8) - ND set
nrow(GWAS_Data_ND[GWAS_Data_ND$P.VALUE<=5*10^-8,])/nrow(GWAS_Data_ND)
GWAS_Data_ND<-GWAS_Data_ND[GWAS_Data_ND$P.VALUE<=5*10^-8,]
## How many associations are there?
nrow(GWAS_Data_ND)
```

```{r basic stats - full, include=TRUE}
# How many unique chromosomes are in the GWAS data?
sum(table(GWAS_Data$CHR_ID))

# Filter out those with no chromosome information. I may need to come back and reconsider this.
GWAS_Data<-GWAS_Data[GWAS_Data$CHR_ID!="",]

# Check unique chromosomes again
sum(table(GWAS_Data$CHR_ID))

# Filter out those with non-standard values (only keep ones where CHR_ID is c(1:22, X, Y))
GWAS_Data<-GWAS_Data[GWAS_Data$CHR_ID %in% c(1:22, "X", "Y"),]

# Check unique chromosomes again
sum(table(GWAS_Data$CHR_ID))

# Convert number chromosomes to string format: "1" to "chr1"
GWAS_Data$CHR_ID<-paste("chr", GWAS_Data$CHR_ID, sep="")

# Check unique chromosomes again
sum(table(GWAS_Data$CHR_ID))

# Make CHR_ID a factor
GWAS_Data$CHR_ID<-factor(GWAS_Data$CHR_ID, levels=paste("chr", c(1:22, "X", "Y"), sep=""))

# Convert CHR_POS to numeric
GWAS_Data$CHR_POS<-as.numeric(GWAS_Data$CHR_POS)

# How many unique chromosomes are in the ND GWAS data?
sum(table(GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID))

# Filter out those with no chromosome information. I may need to come back and reconsider this.
GWAS_Data_ND<-GWAS_Data_ND[GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID!="",]

# Check unique chromosomes again
sum(table(GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID))

# Filter out those with non-standard values (only keep ones where CHR_ID is c(1:22, X, Y))
GWAS_Data_ND<-GWAS_Data_ND[GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID %in% c(1:22, "X", "Y"),]

# Check unique chromosomes again
sum(table(GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID))

# Convert number chromosomes to string format: "1" to "chr1"
GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID<-paste("chr", GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID, sep="")

# Check unique chromosomes again
sum(table(GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID))

# Make CHR_ID a factor
GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID<-factor(GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_ID, levels=paste("chr", c(1:22, "X", "Y"), sep=""))

# Convert CHR_POS to numeric
GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_POS<-as.numeric(GWAS_Data_ND$CHR_POS)

# Combine all associations based on STUDY.ACCESSION
## Change trait to ND
GWAS_Data_ND$MAPPED_TRAIT<-"Neurodegenerative disorders"
GWAS_Data<-rbind(GWAS_Data, GWAS_Data_ND)
## How many associations are there?
nrow(GWAS_Data)
```
```{r extract number of loci per trait, include=TRUE}
# Use grep function to identify the Random traits
NumberLociPerTrait_real<-data.frame(table(GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT[!grepl("Random", GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT)]))

# plot histogram of number of loci per trait - all real observations
ggplot(NumberLociPerTrait_real, aes(x=Freq))+
  geom_histogram(fill="blue", color="black", bins=100)+
  labs(title="Number of loci per trait", x="Number of loci", y="Frequency")+
  CS.THEME+
  scale_x_continuous(trans="log2")+
  geom_vline(xintercept=5, color="red", linetype="dashed")+
  geom_vline(xintercept=nrow(subset(GWAS_Data, MAPPED_TRAIT=="Neurodegenerative disorders")), color="black", linetype="dashed")

# plot histogram of number of loci per trait - all real observations
## This is a place holder for now, as I don't know how to do it quite yet

```

```{r filter out traits with less than 5 loci, include=TRUE}
# how many traits before filtering?
length(unique(GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT))

# Filter out traits with less than 5 loci
NumberLociPerTrait<-data.frame(table(GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT))
GWAS_Data<-GWAS_Data[GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT %in% NumberLociPerTrait[NumberLociPerTrait$Freq>=5,]$Var1,]

# how many traits are left?
length(unique(GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT))
```

```{r define randomization function}
# Define function to generate random sampling of loci
## The function will take in a dataframe of GWAS data. {GWAS_OBJECT}
## It will take in a number of sample, this corresponds to number of loci for the trait of interest {NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES}
## It will take in a index number, which corresponds to the round of randomization we are on {INDEX}
## It will return a dataframe of the same structure as the input dataframe. The MAPPED_TRAIT field will be renamed to Random_{INDEX}.
## The sampling will be done in a trait-wise manner. So instead of randomly pulling rows, it randomly selects a trait adds it's loci, and based on the number of loci for that trait, it randomly selects another trait and adds it's loci. This is done until the number of loci from all sampled traits is equal to the number of loci for the trait of interest.

randomize_gwas_data<-function(GWAS_OBJECT, NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES, INDEX){
  # Initialize object to store random sampling
  RandomSampling<-data.frame()
  
  # Determine the number of loci per unique trait
  NumberLociPerTrait<-data.frame(table(GWAS_OBJECT$MAPPED_TRAIT))
  
  # Shuffle the column order of the NumberLociPerTrait object
  NumberLociPerTrait<-NumberLociPerTrait[sample(1:nrow(NumberLociPerTrait)),]
  
  # Add a column to store the cumulative sum of loci. The first entry should be the number of loci for the first trait. The second entry should be the sum of the first and second trait, and so on.
  NumberLociPerTrait$CumulativeSum<-cumsum(NumberLociPerTrait$Freq)
  
  # Identify the row number where the cumulative sum is greater than or equal to the number of samples
  RowNumber<-which(NumberLociPerTrait$CumulativeSum>=NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES)[1]
  
  # Subset out the traits that will be sampled
  TraitsToSample<-NumberLociPerTrait[1:RowNumber,]$Var1
  
  # Subset out the GWAS data for the traits that will be sampled
  
  GWAS_OBJECT<-GWAS_OBJECT[GWAS_OBJECT$MAPPED_TRAIT %in% TraitsToSample,]
  
  # Change mapped trait field to Random_{INDEX}
  GWAS_OBJECT$MAPPED_TRAIT<-paste("Random_", INDEX, sep="")
  
  # Randomly sample the number of samples from the GWAS data
  RandomSampling<-GWAS_OBJECT[sample(1:nrow(GWAS_OBJECT), NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES, replace=FALSE),]
  
  # Return the object
  return(RandomSampling)
}
```

```{r define master randomization fucntion}
runTraitRandomization<-function(GWAS_OBJECT, TRAIT, ITERATIONS=1000){
  # Determine the number of loci for the trait of interest
  NumberToSample<-nrow(GWAS_OBJECT[GWAS_OBJECT$MAPPED_TRAIT==TRAIT,])
  
  # Generate random sampling sets
  GWAS_Random<-lapply(1:ITERATIONS, function(i) randomize_gwas_data(GWAS_OBJECT, NumberToSample, i)) %>% bind_rows()
  
  # Add TRAIT as suffix to MAPPED_TRAIT of GWAS_Random
  GWAS_Random$MAPPED_TRAIT<-paste(GWAS_Random$MAPPED_TRAIT, TRAIT, sep="_")
  
  # Return the object
  return(GWAS_Random)
}
```

```{r Generate all random samplings}
# Define set of unique traits to test
TraitsToSample<-unique(GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT)

# Generate random sampling sets
GWAS_Random<-lapply(TraitsToSample[c(13,26,64,length(TraitsToSample))], function(TRAIT) runTraitRandomization(GWAS_Data, TRAIT, 50)) %>% bind_rows()
```

```{r combine with GWAS data, include=TRUE}
# Add random sampling sets to GWAS data
GWAS_Data<-rbind(GWAS_Data, GWAS_Random)
rm(GWAS_Random)
```

```{r define functions, include=TRUE}
# Define function to extract diversity metric for each locus
pull_diversity_metric<-function(Chr, position, DiversityObject){
  # Ensure Chr is a string and position is numeric
  Chr<-as.character(Chr)
  position<-as.numeric(as.character(position))
  
  # Extract diversity metric for each locus
  DiversityMetric<-DiversityObject[DiversityObject$Chr==Chr & DiversityObject$Start<=position & DiversityObject$Stop>=position,]
  return(DiversityMetric)
}

# Define function to extract and combine diversity metrics for each locus in an object
pull_diversity_metrics<-function(GWAS_Object, DiversityObject, verbose=FALSE){
  if(verbose){
    print(GWAS_Object$MAPPED_TRAIT[1])
  }
  
  # Initialize object to store diversity metrics
  DiversityMetrics<-data.frame()
  
  # Loop through each locus in the GWAS data
  for(i in 1:nrow(GWAS_Object)){
    # Extract diversity metric for each locus
    DiversityMetric<-pull_diversity_metric(GWAS_Object$CHR_ID[i], GWAS_Object$CHR_POS[i], DiversityObject)
    
    # Add diversity metrics and locus information to object
    DiversityMetric<-cbind(GWAS_Object[i,], DiversityMetric)
    
    # Combine diversity metrics for each locus
    DiversityMetrics<-rbind(DiversityMetrics, DiversityMetric)
  }
  
  return(DiversityMetrics)
}

# Define function to calculate calculate polymorphism count table per object
generate_polymorphism_count_table<-function(GWASDiversityObject, lowerThreshold=-DiversirtyThreshold, upperThreshold=DiversirtyThreshold){
  
  Low<-length(subset(GWASDiversityObject, NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation < lowerThreshold)$NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation)
  Moderate<-length(subset(GWASDiversityObject, NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation >= lowerThreshold & NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation <= upperThreshold)$NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation)
  High<-length(subset(GWASDiversityObject, NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation > upperThreshold)$NonRefAlleles.sum.Deviation)
  Total<-Low+Moderate+High
  
  PolymorphismCountTable<-data.frame(Low=Low, Moderate=Moderate, High=High, Total=Total)
  return(PolymorphismCountTable)
}
```

```{r extract diversity metric for each locus, include=TRUE}
# Extract diversity metrics for each locus in the GWAS data
GWAS_Data_withMetrics<-lapply(unique(GWAS_Data$MAPPED_TRAIT),
       function(TRAIT) pull_diversity_metrics(subset(GWAS_Data, MAPPED_TRAIT==TRAIT), DiversityMetrics.24Kb, verbose=TRUE)
         ) %>% bind_rows()
```

```{r generate polymorphism count table, include=TRUE}
PolymorphismCountTable<-lapply(unique(GWAS_Data_withMetrics$MAPPED_TRAIT),
       function(TRAIT) generate_polymorphism_count_table(subset(GWAS_Data_withMetrics, MAPPED_TRAIT==TRAIT))
       ) %>% bind_rows()

PolymorphismCountTable$Trait<-unique(GWAS_Data_withMetrics$MAPPED_TRAIT)

# Calculate high and low ratio
PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio<-PolymorphismCountTable$High/PolymorphismCountTable$Moderate
PolymorphismCountTable$LowRatio<-PolymorphismCountTable$Low/PolymorphismCountTable$Moderate
```

```{r check for infinite values, include=TRUE}
# Check for infinite values - High ratio
PolymorphismCountTable[is.infinite(PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio),]
# Filter out infinite values
PolymorphismCountTable<-PolymorphismCountTable[!is.infinite(PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio),]

# Check for infinite values - Low ratio
PolymorphismCountTable[is.infinite(PolymorphismCountTable$LowRatio),]
# Filter out infinite values
PolymorphismCountTable<-PolymorphismCountTable[!is.infinite(PolymorphismCountTable$LowRatio),]
```

```{r Subset out the Random traits}
# Subset out the Random traits
PolymorphismCountTable_Random<-PolymorphismCountTable[grepl("Random", PolymorphismCountTable$Trait),]
PolymorphismCountTable<-PolymorphismCountTable[!grepl("Random", PolymorphismCountTable$Trait),]

# Save R data
save(PolymorphismCountTable, file="PolymorphismCountTable.RData")
save(PolymorphismCountTable_Random, file="PolymorphismCountTable_Random.RData")
```

```{r define polymorphism count table plotting function}

plotNullValueDistributions <- function(TRAIT, POLYCOUNTOBJECT, POLYCOUNTOBJECT.RANDOM) {
  PolymorphismCountTable_Random_Subset <- POLYCOUNTOBJECT.RANDOM[grepl(TRAIT, POLYCOUNTOBJECT.RANDOM$Trait),]
  PolymorphismCountTable_Subset <- subset(POLYCOUNTOBJECT, Trait == TRAIT)

  # Calculate upper 5% thresholds for High and Low ratios
  HighRatioThreshold <- quantile(PolymorphismCountTable_Random_Subset$HighRatio, 0.95)
  LowRatioThreshold <- quantile(PolymorphismCountTable_Random_Subset$LowRatio, 0.95)

  # Plot random traits High ratio - histogram
  piece_A <- ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Random_Subset, aes(x = HighRatio)) +
    geom_histogram(fill = "grey", color = "grey") +
    labs(subtitle = "\nHigh ratio",
         x = expression(frac("# Loci in high polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")),
         y = "Frequency") +
    CS.THEME +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6, angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = HighRatioThreshold, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = PolymorphismCountTable_Subset$HighRatio, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed")

  # Plot random traits Low ratio - histogram
  piece_B <- ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Random_Subset, aes(x = LowRatio)) +
    geom_histogram(fill = "grey", color = "grey") +
    labs(subtitle = "\nLow ratio",
         x = expression(frac("# Loci in low polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")),
         y = "Frequency") +
    CS.THEME +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6, angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = LowRatioThreshold, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = PolymorphismCountTable_Subset$LowRatio, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed")

  # Combine plots into a grid and label with the TRAIT
  final_plot <- plot_grid(piece_A, piece_B, ncol = 2, labels = TRAIT, label_size = 20, vjust=1, hjust = -0.1)

  # Display the final plot
  print(final_plot)
}
```

```{r plot polymorphism count table, include=TRUE, fig.width=8, fig.height=3.5}
# Load R data
load("PolymorphismCountTable.RData")
load("PolymorphismCountTable_Random.RData")

# For each trait, run the plotNullValueDistributions function. Combine the resulting plot into a panel
for (TRAIT in TraitsToSample[c(13,26,64,length(TraitsToSample))]) {
  plotNullValueDistributions(TRAIT, PolymorphismCountTable, PolymorphismCountTable_Random)
}
```



```{r plot polymorphism count table, include=TRUE, fig.width=4, fig.height=4}

# Plot Low ratio against total number of loci per trait
## This is plotted for all traits together, but they cannot really be interpreted together deeply

# Plot distribution of High ratio - histogram
piece_C<-ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset, aes(x=HighRatio))+
  geom_histogram(fill="blue", color="blue", binwidth = 0.05)+
  labs(title="High ratio", x=expression(frac("# Loci in high polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")), y="Frequency")+
  CS.THEME+
  # make intervals 0.1
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0, max(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset$HighRatio, na.rm = TRUE), by=0.25))+
  # make x-axis text smaller and rotate
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=6, angle=45, hjust=1))+
  geom_vline(xintercept=HighRatioThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")

# Plot distribution of Low ratio - histogram
piece_E<-ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset, aes(x=LowRatio))+
  geom_histogram(fill="blue", color="blue", binwidth = 0.05)+
  labs(title="Low ratio", x=expression(frac("# Loci in low polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")), y="Frequency")+
  CS.THEME+
  # make intervals 0.1
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0, max(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset$LowRatio, na.rm = TRUE), by=0.25))+
  # make x-axis text smaller and rotate
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=6, angle=45, hjust=1))+
  geom_vline(xintercept=LowRatioThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")

# Plot High ratio against total number of loci per trait
piece_D<-ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset, aes(x=Total, y=HighRatio))+
  geom_point(size=1, stroke=0)+
  labs(title="High ratio vs Total loci per trait", x="Total loci per trait", y=expression(frac("# Loci in high polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")))+
  CS.THEME+
  scale_x_continuous(trans="log2")+
  geom_hline(yintercept=HighRatioThreshold, color="red", linetype="dashed")

piece_F<-ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset, aes(x=Total, y=LowRatio))+
  geom_point(size=1, stroke=0)+
  labs(title="Low ratio vs Total loci per trait", x="Total loci per trait", y=expression(frac("# Loci in low polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")))+
  CS.THEME+
  scale_x_continuous(trans="log2")+
  geom_hline(yintercept=LowRatioThreshold, color="red", linetype="dashed")

# Plot distribution of High ratio - cumulative distribution
piece_G<-ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset, aes(x=HighRatio))+
  stat_ecdf(geom="step", color="blue")+
  labs(title="High ratio", x=expression(frac("# Loci in high polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")), y="Cumulative frequency")+
  CS.THEME+
  # make intervals 0.1
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0, max(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset$HighRatio, na.rm = TRUE), by=0.25))+
  # make x-axis text smaller and rotate
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=6, angle=45, hjust=1))+
  geom_vline(xintercept=HighRatioThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")

# Plot distribution of Low ratio - cumulative distribution
piece_H<-ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset, aes(x=LowRatio))+
  stat_ecdf(geom="step", color="blue")+
  labs(title="Low ratio", x=expression(frac("# Loci in low polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")), y="Cumulative frequency")+
  CS.THEME+
  # make intervals 0.1
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(0, max(PolymorphismCountTable_Subset$LowRatio, na.rm = TRUE), by=0.25))+
  # make x-axis text smaller and rotate
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=6, angle=45, hjust=1))+
  geom_vline(xintercept=LowRatioThreshold, color="black", linetype="dashed")

```

```{r Plot High vs Low ratio, include=TRUE}
# Plot High vs Low ratio
ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable, aes(x=LowRatio, y=HighRatio)) +
  geom_point(size=1, stroke=0) +
  labs(
    title = "High vs Low ratio",
    x = expression(frac("# Loci in low polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")),
    y = expression(frac("# Loci in high polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions"))
  ) +
  CS.THEME +
  geom_hline(yintercept = HighRatioThreshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = LowRatioThreshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed")

subset(PolymorphismCountTable, HighRatio>=HighRatioThreshold)
```
```{r fit distribution to Random HighRatio data, include=TRUE}
# library(MASS)
# # gammma distribution to Random HighRatio data
# 
# ## Confirm numerical requirements of beta distribution (Should all return 0)
# sum(!is.finite(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio))
# sum(is.na(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio))
# sum(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio<=0)
# sum(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio>=1)
# 
# ## Calculate mean and variance to calculate starting parameters
# BetaMean<-mean(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio)
# BetaVar<-var(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio)
# 
# ## Calculate starting parameters
# InitialShape1<-BetaMean*(BetaMean*(1-BetaMean)/BetaVar-1)
# InitialShape2<-(1-BetaMean)*(BetaMean*(1-BetaMean)/BetaVar-1)
# 
# ## Fit beta distribution to Random HighRatio data
# #BetaModelFit<-fitdistr(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio, dbeta, start=list(shape1=InitialShape1, shape2=InitialShape2))
# 
# BetaModelFit<-fitdistr(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio, dgamma, start=list(shape1=1, rate=0.1))
# 
# BetaModelFit <- fitdistr(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio, "gamma", start = list(shape = 1, rate = 0.1))
# 
# 
# BetaModelFit
# 
# # Create histogram with fitted beta curve
# ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable_Random, aes(x = HighRatio)) +
#   geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), bins = 30, fill = "gray", color = "black") +
#   stat_function(fun = dbeta, args = list(shape1 = BetaModelFit$estimate[1], shape2 = BetaModelFit$estimate[2]), 
#                 color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
#   labs(title = "Histogram of Randomized High Ratio with Fitted Beta Distribution", 
#        x = "High Ratio", y = "Density") +
#   theme_minimal()
# 
# # Generate quantiles
# observed_quantiles <- sort(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio)
# theoretical_quantiles <- qbeta(ppoints(length(observed_quantiles)), shape1 = BetaModelFit$estimate[1], shape2 = BetaModelFit$estimate[2])
# 
# # Create a data frame for plotting
# qq_data <- data.frame(Theoretical = theoretical_quantiles, Observed = observed_quantiles)
# 
# # Plot QQ plot
# ggplot(qq_data, aes(x = Theoretical, y = Observed)) +
#   geom_point(color = "black") +
#   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
#   labs(title = "QQ Plot of High Ratio vs. Fitted Beta Distribution",
#        x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Observed Quantiles") +
#   theme_minimal()
```

```{r estimate High ratio significance, include=TRUE}
# Calculate p-values for High ratio
#PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio_p.value<-1-pbeta(PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio, BetaModelFit$estimate[1], BetaModelFit$estimate[2])
## Use empirical p-value
PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio_p.value<-1-ecdf(PolymorphismCountTable_Random$HighRatio)(PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio)

# Plot distribution of p-values
ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable, aes(x=HighRatio_p.value))+
  geom_histogram(fill="blue", color="blue", bins=100)+
  labs(title="Distribution of p-values for High ratio", x="p-value", y="Frequency")+
  CS.THEME

# MTC correction
#library(qvalue)
#MTC_HighRatio<-qvalue(PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio_p.value)
## Plot q-value histogram
#plot(MTC_HighRatio)

## Add q-values to PolymorphismCountTable
#PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio_q.value<-MTC_HighRatio$qvalues

## BH FDR correction
PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio_FDR<-p.adjust(PolymorphismCountTable$HighRatio_p.value, method="BH")

# Plot High ratio FDR vs q-values
# ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable, aes(x=HighRatio_FDR, y=HighRatio_q.value)) +
#   geom_point(size=1, stroke=0) +
#   labs(
#     title = "High ratio FDR vs q-values (jittered)",
#     x = "FDR",
#     y = "q-value"
#   ) +
#   CS.THEME +
#   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, color = "red", linetype = "dashed")+
#   geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, color = "red", linetype = "dashed")
```

```{r plot High ratio against fdr values, include=TRUE}
# Plot High ratio against FDR
ggplot(PolymorphismCountTable, aes(x=HighRatio, y=HighRatio_FDR)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1, stroke=0, width=0.1, height=0.01, alpha=0.75) +
  labs(
    title = "High ratio vs FDR values (jittered)",
    x = expression(frac("# Loci in high polymorphic regions", "# Loci in medium polymorphic regions")),
    y = "FDR value"
  ) +
  CS.THEME +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = HighRatioThreshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed")
```

```{r extract traits enriched for polymorphic regions, include=TRUE}
# How many traits total?
nrow(PolymorphismCountTable)

# How many traits have High ratio above threshold and with FDR<0.05?
nrow(subset(PolymorphismCountTable, HighRatio>HighRatioThreshold & HighRatio_FDR<0.05))

# What traits have High ratio above threshold and with FDR<0.05?
subset(PolymorphismCountTable, HighRatio>HighRatioThreshold & HighRatio_FDR<0.05)

subset(PolymorphismCountTable, Trait=="Neurodegenerative disorders")

# Save PolymorphismCountTable
write.table(PolymorphismCountTable, "PolymorphismCountTable.tsv", sep="\t", quote=FALSE, row.names=FALSE)
```
